- - """
- - $$$$$_BEGIN_TEST_METADATA_DECLARATION_$$$$$

- - """
- - Maps tray into device id and the other way around.
- - Tray1 should map into deviceId 1
- - Tray2 should map into deviceId 2
- - Tray3 should map into deviceId 3
- - """
- - trayToDeviceId_ = { 1: 1, 2: 2, 3: 3 }
- - deviceIdToTray_ = { 1: 1, 2: 2, 3: 3 }

- -
- - """
- - Sends a plot into the printer and returns the job ID.
- -
- - Args:
- -     net: Network object for communication.
- -     udw: User-defined wrapper for job management.
- -     print_path: Path to the print file.
- -
- - Returns:
- -     job_id: The ID of the sent job.
- - """
- - def send_job(net, udw, print_path):
- -
- -     # Get the "last" job ID
- -     old_job_id = udw.mainApp.JobManager.getRecentJobs().split('\n')[-1]
- -
- -     # Ensure the returned job ID is an integer
- -     try:
- -         int(old_job_id)
- -     except ValueError:
- -         pytest.fail("Job ID is not a valid integer")
- -
- -     # Send the job
- -     results = {}
- -     print_job = DunePrintJob(net.ip_address, 9100)
- -     print_job.send_job(
- -         print_path, noHeaders=True, verbose=False, asyncPrint=False, resultData=results
- -     )
- -
- -     # Wait for the job ID to update
- -     start_time = time.monotonic()
- -     max_wait_time = 5.0
- -     job_id = udw.mainApp.JobManager.getRecentJobs().split('\n')[-1]
- -
- -     logging.info("\n\nWaiting for job ID to update...")
- -     while job_id == old_job_id and (time.monotonic() - start_time) < max_wait_time:
- -         time.sleep(0.01)
- -         job_id = udw.mainApp.JobManager.getRecentJobs().split('\n')[-1]
- -
- -     # If job ID didn't change, we timed out
- -     if job_id == old_job_id:
- -         logging.info(
- -             f"After waiting {round(time.monotonic() - start_time, 2)}, "
- -             f"jobID={job_id} didn't update"
- -         )
- -         assert old_job_id != job_id
- -     else:
- -         logging.info(f"New job ID = {job_id}\n")
- -
- -     # Verify the job state history contains 'READY'
- -     job_state = udw.mainApp.JobManager.getJobDetails(job_id)
- -     assert 'READY' in job_state
- -     logging.info("Job state: READY")
- -
- -     return job_id
- -
- - """
- - Waits for the job to be in the desired status
- - """
- - def wait_job_in_status(jobId, udw, statusString, maxPrintTime = 100):

- -     startTime = time.monotonic()

- -     jobstate = udw.mainApp.JobManager.getJobDetails(jobId)

- -

- -         assert(position >= 0)

- - def is_multiple_size_type_prompts_feature_supported(cdm):
- -     """
- -     Checks if the multiple size type prompts feature is supported.
- -     Parameters:
- -     - cdm: The CDM object used to make the API request.
- -     Returns:
- -     - True if the multiple size type prompts feature is supported, False otherwise.
- -     """
- -     alert_response = cdm.get_raw(cdm.CDM_MEDIAHANDLING_ALERTS)
- -     alert_response_body = alert_response.json()
- -
- -     index = -1
- -     for i in range(len(alert_response_body['alerts'])):
- -         if alert_response_body['alerts'][i]['category'] == "sizeType":
- -             index = i
- -             break
- -
- -     if alert_response_body['alerts'][index]['stringId'] == 81080: # Enterprise I/O ref that indicates multiple size type prompts are supported
- -         logging.info("Multiple size type prompts feature is supported")
- -         assert alert_response_body['alerts'][index]['severity'] == 'information'
- -         return True
- -
- -     return False

- - """
- - Send a plot into the printer.
- - Returns the id of the job
- - """
- - def sendJob(net, udw, PRINTPATH):
- -
- -     # Get the "last" job ID.  Even if no job existed since boot, this
- -     # will still return a valid integer.
- -     oldJobId = udw.mainApp.JobManager.getRecentJobs().split('\n')[-1]
- -     # Make sure returned job ID was an integer (albeit, in string form)
- -     try:
- -         intVal = int(oldJobId)
- -     except ValueError:
- -         pytest.fail("Job ID is not a valid integer")
- -
- -     # When printing asynchronously, this dictionary can be used to determine
- -     # the state and send progress of the printing thread
- -     results = {}
- -
- -     printjob = DunePrintJob(net.ip_address, 9100)
- -
- -     printjob.send_job(PRINTPATH, noHeaders=True, verbose=False,
- -                       asyncPrint=False, resultData=results)
- -
- -     # Now, wait for the job ID to update to a new value
- -     startTime = time.monotonic()
- -     timeSpentWaiting = time.monotonic() - startTime
- -     maxJobIdChangeWait = 5.0
- -
- -     jobId = udw.mainApp.JobManager.getRecentJobs().split('\n')[-1]
- -
- -     print("\n\nWaiting for job ID to update...")
- -
- -     while (jobId == oldJobId) and (timeSpentWaiting < maxJobIdChangeWait):
- -         time.sleep(0.01)
- -         jobId = udw.mainApp.JobManager.getRecentJobs().split('\n')[-1]
- -         timeSpentWaiting = time.monotonic() - startTime
- -
